BoxedPrimitiveEquality.checker_desc: Detects equality comparisons (== or !=) where both operands are boxed primitive types, which may incorrectly use reference equality instead of value equality.

LogicalAssignment.checker_desc: This checker detects assignments used as boolean conditions in control flow statements (if, while, do-while, for) where a boolean expression is expected.

NarrowingCompoundAssignment.checker_desc: This checker detects compound assignment operations where the right-hand side expression's type is not convertible to the left-hand side variable's primitive type, potentially hiding a lossy cast.

MissingCasesInEnumSwitch.checker_desc: Detects switch statements or expressions on enum types that do not handle all possible enum values and lack a default case (or have a default case that is marked as a skew comment).

FragmentInjection.checker_desc: Detects classes extending android.preference.PreferenceActivity that either do not implement the isValidFragment method or implement it in a way that unconditionally returns true.

EqualsIncompatibleType.checker_desc: This checker detects equality comparisons (via equals methods, static Objects.equals, or Predicate.isEqual) between two types that are provably incompatible, meaning the comparison will always evaluate to false.

InterruptedExceptionSwallowed.checker_desc: This checker detects catch blocks that catch a supertype of InterruptedException (like Exception or Throwable) without handling the interruption separately, and methods that can throw InterruptedException but declare a broader throws clause.

DoubleCheckedLocking.checker_desc: Detects unsafe double-checked locking patterns where a non-volatile field is read outside synchronized blocks after a null-check.

DereferenceWithNullBranch.checker_desc: This checker detects member select (field/method access) expressions where the receiver expression has a branch that is definitely null, leading to a potential null dereference.

RedundantNullCheck.checker_desc: Detects redundant null checks on expressions that are statically determined to be non-null based on language semantics or nullness annotations.

MissingBraces.checker_desc: This checker detects if, else, for, do, and while statements whose body is not a block (i.e., missing braces).

IntLongMath.checker_desc: Detects when an int-typed arithmetic expression may overflow before being assigned to a long-typed variable, field, or return value.

EqualsHashCode.checker_desc: Detects classes that override equals(Object) in a non-trivial way but do not override hashCode().

MissingDefault.checker_desc: Detects switch statements missing a default case when not exhaustive or having an empty default case without a comment.

ReturnValueIgnored.checker_desc: This checker detects method calls, constructor invocations, or method references where the return value is ignored, but the method is annotated or belongs to a class/method category where the return value is expected to be used.

FallThrough.checker_desc: Detects switch cases where execution may unintentionally fall through to the next case without a proper "fall through" comment, or where a "fall through" comment exists but fall-through is impossible.

ReferenceEquality.checker_desc: This checker detects comparisons using reference equality (== or !=) on objects that have value-based equality semantics (i.e., they override equals).

OperatorPrecedence.checker_desc: This checker detects binary expressions or conditional expressions where operator precedence is potentially confusing and suggests adding parentheses for clarity.

SynchronizeOnNonFinalField.checker_desc: This checker detects synchronization on a non-final field that is not safely exempted by being local, static, final, lazily initialized, or belonging to specific I/O classes.

ToStringReturnsNull.checker_desc: Detects when a toString() method implementation returns a null literal directly or within parentheses or a conditional expression.

UnusedLabel.checker_desc: This checker detects labeled statements whose label is never referenced by any break or continue statement within the same compilation unit.

LockOnBoxedPrimitive.checker_desc: Detects synchronization on boxed primitive objects or calling wait/notify/notifyAll methods on boxed primitive receivers, which can lead to unintended lock sharing.

UnsynchronizedOverridesSynchronized.checker_desc: This checker detects unsynchronized methods that override a synchronized method in a superclass, unless the override is in java.io.InputStream or the method body consists solely of a super call to the overridden method or a constant return.

SystemExitOutsideMain.checker_desc: Detects calls to System.exit() that are not inside a main method and where the enclosing class does not contain any main method.

ShortCircuitBoolean.checker_desc: This checker detects uses of the non-short-circuiting boolean operators `&` and `|` where the short-circuiting operators `&&` and `||` could be used instead.
